summary: s7ae.1 shipped a LIVE-LOCAL coordination envelope (git + OS process table + bd + filesystem handoff), but its design/acceptance still claim archive-evidence composition (topology_edges, session/run events, tool/action blocks, proof/outcome, context-flow) that the code never implements — the archive is touched only for a PRAGMA user_version schema-health probe (envelope.py:552-579). Resolution is BOTH: narrow s7ae.1 to the shipped reality so its AC is not falsely counted satisfied, AND add a new epic-AC-implied child that actually composes the archive read-evidence (distinct from s7ae.3 messages and s7ae.2 MCP/hook predeploy). Separately, the epic's headline two-agent live-proof acceptance line has no owning child and is finalized here.

updates.set_design: Shipped shape (polylogue/coordination/envelope.py, payloads.py, rendering.py; polylogue/cli/commands/agents.py; polylogue/mcp/server_tools.py:agent_coordination). The envelope is composed from LIVE LOCAL evidence, not archive-history joins: (1) git discovery via `git -C rev-parse --show-toplevel`, `branch --show-current`, `rev-parse HEAD`, `status --porcelain=v1` for repo root/branch/head/dirty/changed_paths; (2) current-process env + sys.argv for self / agent-kind inference (_infer_agent_kind); (3) the OS process table (`ps -eo pid,ppid,comm,args`) for peer agent processes (codex/claude/gemini) and generic resource episodes (polylogued/pytest/python/uv/nix/cargo/rustc/node) with heuristic classification and /proc/<pid>/cwd for peer cwd; (4) Beads adapters when `.beads` exists: `bd list --status=in_progress` (work item), `bd hooks list`, `bd gate list`, `bd merge-slot check`; (5) filesystem handoff refs under `.agent/conductor-devloop/{ACTIVE-LOOP,HANDOFF-LATEST,OPERATING-LOG}.md`; (6) an ARCHIVE SCHEMA-HEALTH PROBE ONLY (_archive_payload): archive_root()/active_index_db_path() presence plus PRAGMA user_version on index/source/user tiers and daemon-process liveness filtered from the process table — this is NOT archive-evidence composition. Overlaps (same-repo-agent, resource-episode, dirty-unclaimed-work) are derived from the process table + git and are awareness, not blockers. CLI: `polylogue agents {status,self,work-item,current,conflicts,overlap,handoff}` with `--json` as the primary contract and a bounded view projection (project_coordination_envelope) that trims non-relevant arrays per view; markdown/tree via rendering.py. MCP: the `agent_coordination` tool + prompt return the same typed payload. Every inferred field carries provenance/confidence/freshness. EXPLICITLY OUT OF SCOPE for this bead and deferred: joining topology_edges, run/session events, tool/action blocks, proof/outcome summaries, and context-flow refs from the archive is tracked by the new archive session-evidence composition child; coordination messages/advisories are s7ae.3; the web mission-control renderer (bby.9) is a separate projection.
updates.set_acceptance: Typed Coordination* payload models and build_coordination_envelope() exist under polylogue/coordination/. CLI `polylogue agents` exposes status/self/work-item/current/conflicts/overlap/handoff with stable JSON and bounded output, and project_coordination_envelope() trims non-relevant arrays per view. MCP exposes the `agent_coordination` tool + prompt returning the same payload (registered in EXPECTED_TOOL_NAMES / TOOL_CONTRACT). Beads enriches the work item plus hooks/gates/merge-slot when `.beads` is present; a no-Beads repo returns git/process-derived coordination state. Peers, resource episodes, and overlaps derive from the OS process table + git; the archive is touched ONLY for schema-health (PRAGMA user_version) and daemon liveness. Tests cover Beads-present and Beads-absent paths, view-projection bounding, and the MCP shared-payload contract (tests/unit/coordination/test_envelope.py, tests/unit/mcp/test_agent_coordination.py). NOT claimed by this bead (tracked separately): archive session-tree/topology/proof/context-flow composition (new archive-composition child) and coordination messages/advisories (s7ae.3). bby.9 web mission-control remains a separate renderer over this envelope.

new_beads.title: Compose archive session-tree/topology/proof/context-flow evidence into coordination envelope
new_beads.design: Extend build_coordination_envelope (polylogue/coordination/envelope.py) with the archive-evidence composition layer that s7ae.1's original design promised but the shipped envelope never implemented: today _archive_payload (envelope.py:552-579) only probes PRAGMA user_version on index/source/user tiers plus daemon-process liveness. Add bounded read joins over the active index.db using existing repository/query machinery (polylogue/storage/repository, api/__init__.py) rather than hand-rolled SQL where a reader exists: (1) active/historical agent SESSION TREES for the current repo/worktree/branch from sessions + topology_edges (get_session_topology / get_logical_session), keyed off cwd / POLYLOGUE_SESSION_REF; (2) recent run/session EVENTS and tool/action BLOCKS as archive-derived activity episodes that enrich or corroborate the live process-table resource episodes; (3) PROOF/OUTCOME summaries (postmortem / pathology read models) as bounded refs; (4) CONTEXT-FLOW refs from the context compiler/ledger. Each composed field is a bounded, ref-first projection carrying provenance/freshness/confidence with an explicit degrade order (full -> ref-only -> drop). Add CoordinationSessionTree / CoordinationActivityEpisode / CoordinationProofRef / CoordinationContextFlowRef payloads (SurfacePayloadModel) in coordination/payloads.py and wire them into AgentCoordinationPayload + project_coordination_envelope view bounding. Pitfalls: keep it read-only and limit-clamped exactly like the shipped envelope; when index.db is absent/stale, return schema-health-only WITHOUT error (preserve current behavior); do NOT duplicate s7ae.3 coordination-message composition or s7ae.2 MCP/hook wiring.
new_beads.acceptance: build_coordination_envelope composes, when the active index.db is present and current, bounded refs for: the current repo/session-tree lineage (sessions + topology_edges), recent activity/tool-action episodes, proof/outcome summaries, and context-flow refs — each carrying provenance/confidence and a documented degrade order. With no or stale index.db the envelope still returns (schema-health-only, as shipped) without raising. CLI `polylogue agents status`/`current` and the MCP `agent_coordination` payload surface the new fields under bounded arrays clamped by the existing limit. Tests cover archive-present composition, archive-absent degrade, limit/bound enforcement, and provenance presence on every composed field. No overlap with s7ae.3 (messages) or s7ae.2 (MCP/hook predeploy) is introduced. Verify: devtools test tests/unit/coordination tests/unit/mcp/test_agent_coordination.py.

new_beads.title: Live proof: two agents, separate worktrees, one repo — overlap, message, context, handoff
new_beads.design: Realize the epic s7ae HEADLINE acceptance line ('A live proof demonstrates at least two agents on one repo with separate worktrees, visible overlap/resource awareness, a scoped coordination message, context injection, and a handoff packet') — currently owned by no child. Build a reproducible proof (run script + captured JSON artifacts) demonstrating two agents (e.g. Claude + Codex) on ONE repo in SEPARATE git worktrees, showing via the coordination envelope: (a) mutual peer + same-repo-agent + resource-episode awareness (process-table overlaps already shipped in s7ae.1); (b) at least one SCOPED coordination message posted by one agent and observed as delivered/addressed in the other's envelope (s7ae.3); (c) context injection into the second agent recorded via the 37t.11 scheduler/ledger; (d) a handoff packet (.agent/conductor-devloop/*.md) produced by one agent and referenced by both. Implement as a deterministic devtools workspace proof (mirror the existing devtools/workspace proof-artifact pattern, e.g. degraded-archive-proof) plus an operator run script; capture before/after `polylogue agents status --json` envelopes from both agents as the evidence artifact. Pitfalls: overlap is awareness-not-blocker (assert non-blocking); the proof must run without private corpus; the coordination message must actually round-trip through the message store (no fabricated delivery). Also depends on the new archive session-evidence composition child (this batch) so the captured envelope carries session-tree lineage, and on 37t.11 for the context-injection leg.
new_beads.acceptance: A committed, reproducible proof exists (run script + captured before/after JSON envelope artifacts under a devtools workspace path) demonstrating: two agents on one repo in separate worktrees; each envelope shows the other as a same-repo peer with overlap + resource-episode awareness; exactly one scoped coordination message posted and observed as delivered/addressed in the recipient's envelope; context injection recorded via the 37t.11 ledger; a handoff packet produced and referenced by both agents. The proof runs from one documented command and the epic s7ae live-proof acceptance line is explicitly marked satisfied by this artifact. Deps: s7ae.1 (envelope/overlap/handoff), s7ae.3 (scoped message), 37t.11 (context injection), and the archive session-evidence composition child (session-tree lineage).
